root_motivation: PRD-agentic-memory §9.4 surfaces operational gap: /self-review hand-rolls index-vs-file divergence checks because there's no `recall doctor`. The full v0.2 rebuild is multi-session work; this slice ships `recall-doctor` as a standalone companion binary that reads the v0.1 recall data dir, reports {file_count, indexed_count, orphans, missing, embedder_ids, schema_version}, and exits non-zero on divergence. When v0.2 properly rebuilds recall, `doctor` absorbs into the main binary; today it's a useful side-tool that closes the /self-review gap immediately.
user_persona: jsy and /self-review Phase A reading divergence reports between the on-disk Markdown memory store (~/.claude/recall/memories/) and the SQLite index (~/.claude/recall/index/recall.sqlite). Output: JSON consumed by /self-review (and shell-pipeline), text for human terminal use.

acceptance_criteria.description: CLI binary `recall-doctor` accepts `[--root <dir>]` (default `~/.claude/recall`). Reads the directory, prints a divergence report to stdout, exits 0 when in sync. Empty/nonexistent root → exit 0 with empty report.
Walks `<root>/memories/` recursively, counting *.md files; ignores non-Markdown and dot-prefixed directories. Reports `file_count: N`.

Queries `<root>/index/recall.sqlite` `memories_meta` table for row count; reports `indexed_count: N`. Missing DB → `indexed_count: null` and warning. Shells out to `sqlite3` binary; absence of `sqlite3` → reports `indexed_count: null` + warning.
Computes orphans: file IDs present on disk (parsed from frontmatter `id:` field) but not in `memories_meta`. Reports `orphans: [<id>...]` sorted lexicographically.
Computes missing: `memories_meta` rows whose `path` column points to a non-existent file. Reports `missing: [<id>...]` sorted lexicographically.
Reports `embedder_ids: [<id>...]` — distinct `embedding_id` values from `memories_meta` (excluding NULL). Empty list when no rows or no embeddings.

Output format selectable via `--format text|json` (default text). JSON shape: `{file_count, indexed_count, orphans, missing, embedder_ids, schema_version, warnings}`. Deterministic key ordering; same input → byte-identical output.

Exit code 0 when in sync (orphans+missing empty AND indexed_count == file_count). Exit code 1 when divergent. Exit code 2 on invocation error (bad --root that exists but isn't a dir, malformed args). Stable across runs.

`--fix` invokes `recall reindex` (shelling out via PATH) when orphans OR missing is non-empty. Default (no --fix) is read-only — never writes. When `recall` isn't on PATH, `--fix` emits a warning and exits with code 1.

Reports `schema_version: <string>` derived from a `meta` table or `PRAGMA user_version`; falls back to `"v0.1-undeclared"` when no schema version is queryable. Documented in the report so /self-review can warn on bumps.

scope: Rust 2024 CLI crate `recall-doctor` producing a `recall-doctor` binary.
Walker over `<root>/memories/` for *.md files; YAML frontmatter parse for the `id` field.
Shell-out to `sqlite3` binary for indexed_count, missing rows, embedder_ids, schema_version.
Two output formats: human-readable text (default) + stable JSON (--format json).
non_goals: Not recall itself. No write surface to memories, no index rebuild logic in-tree (delegates to `recall reindex`).
Not the full v0.2 rebuild. recall-doctor is a side-tool; the v0.2 binary will absorb it later.
No SQLite linking (no rusqlite). Uses sqlite3 shell-out to keep dep count low.
No daemon, no socket, no network.
No auto-correction of frontmatter (e.g. rewriting bad timestamps); divergence-detection only.
Not a generic SQLite health-check; only the recall v0.1 schema is recognized.

five_whys_trace.q: Why a standalone `recall-doctor` instead of adding `doctor` to the recall binary?
five_whys_trace.a: The recall binary is already deployed and stable in v0.1. Adding doctor inline requires a v0.2 rebuild that's multi-session work. A standalone tool lands the value now; absorption is mechanical later.

five_whys_trace.q: Why shell out to `sqlite3` instead of using rusqlite?
five_whys_trace.a: rusqlite + bundled sqlite is ~6MB of extra binary + license/audit surface. The doctor tool runs once per /self-review pass; shelling out costs ~30ms — negligible. Keeps the binary small + deps under 8.
Different repair paths. Orphans (file-without-index) → reindex picks them up. Missing (index-without-file) → row needs to be deleted from the index. Lumping them obscures which fix to apply.

five_whys_trace.q: Why JSON output as a first-class format?
five_whys_trace.a: /self-review consumes this programmatically. Text format is for human terminal use; JSON is the contract with the consuming skill.

ambiguities_resolved.question: How does doctor know what the canonical schema version is?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Queries `PRAGMA user_version` first; if 0, looks for a `meta` table (future v0.2 convention); if neither, reports `v0.1-undeclared`. This lets v0.2 bump the version cleanly without breaking doctor.

ambiguities_resolved.question: What if `recall reindex` doesn't exist on PATH when --fix is invoked?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Warning to stderr + exit 1. Doctor never tries to rebuild the index itself; that's recall's job exclusively.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Do hidden subdirs under memories/ get walked?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: No, per the linter's precedent. Dot-prefixed directories are skipped — same walker semantics as recall-memory-linter.
